outlook: Product Designer roles show resilience despite market headwinds, with 97,332 open positions available even as demand declined 5.8% year-over-year. The median salary sits at $88,000, reflecting stable compensation in a competitive field. While AI tools like v0, Galileo AI, and Figma AI are reshaping workflows, the moderate risk score of 40/100 suggests designers who adapt will thrive rather than be displaced. The skills evolution tells a clear story: technical software proficiency is becoming commoditized as tools grow more intuitive, while human-centered abilities like active listening, reading comprehension, and complex problem solving are increasingly valuable. Community discussions focus heavily on AI integration rather than job displacement fears, indicating designers are embracing these tools as productivity multipliers rather than viewing them as threats.

skillPivots.headline: Develop strategic problem-solving consultancy
skillPivots.body: Pivot from tool-heavy execution to strategic consulting by mastering Complex Problem Solving and Active Listening to guide cross-functional teams through ambiguous product challenges. Position yourself as the problem-framing expert who defines what to build before AI tools like v0 and Galileo AI generate the solutions.

skillPivots.headline: Master stakeholder alignment and facilitation
skillPivots.body: Since presentation software skills are declining in importance, focus on Active Listening and Reading Comprehension to become an expert facilitator who synthesizes diverse stakeholder needs into clear product requirements. Lead workshops and strategy sessions that human insight is essential for, while AI handles the visual execution.

skillPivots.headline: Transition to AI-augmented design operations
skillPivots.body: Leverage your product design foundation to become a design ops specialist who orchestrates Figma AI, v0, and Galileo AI tools across teams. Use Complex Problem Solving skills to optimize AI-human workflows and establish quality standards for AI-generated design outputs.
